title: Build the Magical Single-Task Loop

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A configured user can start with repository, task, and Run safely.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: SingleTaskPage exposes repository and one multiline task field, keeps all other controls under Details, and the clean-success Playwright scenario passes.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Strongest eligible is the pre-qualification default.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Performance is the shared CLI/Agentd/web default and policy ordering selects the strongest eligible backend first.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Verification is mandatory and no default time budget is invented.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Web and CLI submissions require verification and omit max_wall_seconds unless the user supplies it.
villani.product_run.v1 derives Understanding, Working, Checking, and Ready from persisted canonical events; the four stage screenshots pass.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Result uses one of four verdicts.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: The shared schema limits final_verdict to Ready to apply, Needs review, Could not prove, or Cancelled.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: CLI and web consume the same projection.
No unproved change can be delivered.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: The product contract and delivery workflow expose delivery actions only for selected, proved work and reject stale, drifted, missing-evidence, and accepted-unverified results.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Gate A passes in a machine-readable report.
acceptance_criteria.evidence: docs/PT2_GATE_A.json reports PASS with no blocking or acceptance failures.
PT3 qualification, routing, team, SaaS, and later-milestone behavior were not introduced.

architecture_and_product_decisions: villani.product_run.v1 is the sole public product projection for CLI and web. Acceptance and delivery-action logic live in Python, not in the frontend.
Canonical persisted events, rather than timers, map to exactly four public stages. Retry and escalation language is derived by the same projection.
Ready to apply requires selected acceptance-grade verification evidence. Missing, malformed, accepted-unverified, unselected, stale, or drifted evidence fails closed.
Performance and strongest-eligible-first are pre-qualification defaults. Verification is mandatory, max wall time is absent by default, and delivery is deferred until after the result.
Repository/validation discovery is cached by repository fingerprint and can be cancelled. Submission uses an idempotency key and run status uses event subscription with a bounded polling fallback.

Cancellation is a durable controller state. It requests runner cancellation, blocks future controller work, preserves evidence, performs isolation cleanup, and reports whether the target was modified.
Browser refresh reconnects from the durable run identity. Browser closure has no server-side cancellation side effect.
Unknown cost and duration remain null with explicit accounting status; combined check counts become unknown when an input accounting source is unknown.
The result presentation order is contract-driven: verdict/reason, change summary, files, checks, requirements, cost, duration, one action, then Evidence.

schema_and_configuration_migrations.compatibility: New durable root and packaged schemas plus Python/TypeScript models. Existing bundles without product-run.json are projected conservatively from their canonical artifacts.
The product_run artifact reference is optional for backwards readability.
CANCELLED was added without changing the meaning of existing states.
Performance, mandatory verification, no wall-time cap, and deferred delivery are request defaults; existing explicit advanced flags remain accepted.

tests_added_or_extended: Five product projection tests cover legacy projection, fail-closed proof, retry language, forbidden delivery actions, and combined check accounting.
Controller/protocol tests cover durable cancellation and the additive product artifact/state contracts.
CLI tests cover shared defaults, optional success criteria, four-stage output, JSON parity, Evidence inspection, cancellation, and backwards-compatible advanced flags.

Agentd tests cover repository-fingerprint discovery caching, uncertain/unavailable validation, idempotent multiline submissions, background failures, event subscription, cancellation, evidence preservation, and HTTP routes.
Villani Web unit tests cover drafts, visible-control budget, safe defaults, status reconnection, result ordering/actions, unknown accounting, and cancellation.

Eleven PT2 Playwright scenarios cover clean success, apply, branch, validation retry, missing evidence, cancellation, refresh, dirty target, unknown cost, unavailable agent, and target drift/delivery conflict.
CLI/web projection equality is asserted directly by test_cli_json_equals_the_product_projection_consumed_by_web.

validations.command: $r = Get-Content docs\PT2_COMPLETION_REPORT.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json; $g = Get-Content docs\PT2_GATE_A.json -Raw | ConvertFrom-Json; if ($r.status -ne 'COMPLETE' -or $r.pt3_started -ne $false) { throw 'Completion status mismatch' }; if ($g.status -ne 'PASS' -or $g.pt3_started -ne $false -or $g.blocking_failures.Count -ne 0 -or $g.acceptance_failures.Count -ne 0) { throw 'Gate status mismatch' }; $missing = @(); foreach ($kind in @('added','changed')) { foreach ($path in $r.files.$kind) { if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $path)) { $missing += $path } } }; $present = @(); foreach ($path in $r.files.deleted) { if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $path) { $present += $path } }; if ($missing.Count -ne 0) { throw ('Missing report paths: ' + ($missing -join ', ')) }; if ($present.Count -ne 0) { throw ('Deleted report paths still present: ' + ($present -join ', ')) }; 'completion report consistent: {0} added, {1} changed, {2} deleted; Gate A {3}' -f $r.files.added.Count,$r.files.changed.Count,$r.files.deleted.Count,$g.status
validations.result: PASS: completion report consistent: 30 added, 46 changed, 2 deleted; Gate A PASS. Two preceding Python one-line attempts failed only because PowerShell stripped nested quoting; the JSON parsers had already passed.

scenarios: clean success and all four stages
end_to_end_artifacts.visual_review: All PT2 PNGs were inspected individually, including original-resolution review of the four stages, all verdicts, target-state placement, and 320px New task layout.
end_to_end_artifacts.packaged_parity: Source build and Agentd packaged console digests match for all 3 files; two byte-parity tests passed.

known_failures: No final test, Gate A, or acceptance failure remains.
Sandboxed Vite/Vitest process launches were denied; the same commands passed with the approved process permission.
The in-app Browser connector had no attached browser. Playwright/Chromium provided the required deterministic browser evidence.
The standalone run-model npm build lacks a local tsc binary; pinned repository TypeScript and Vitest binaries compiled and tested it successfully.
A broad mypy run that followed the existing package graph found 202 errors in 39 files. The full eleven-file PT2 production surface passes focused mypy with imports skipped, and package tests pass.
skipped_and_deselected: Villani Ops: 2 host-capability tests skipped and 114 opt-in/configuration tests deselected.
Villani Code: 1 opt-in external Claude Code smoke test skipped.
In-app browser inspection skipped because agent.browsers.list() returned no browsers; no application behavior was skipped from Playwright.
security_privacy_data_loss_compatibility: Delivery remains fail closed and is never offered for unproved, stale, unselected, drifted, or accepted-unverified work.
Cancellation is cooperative before delivery and cleanup is durable. An already-started atomic delivery is not interrupted mid-write; its final target state is reported truthfully.
Drafts are stored only in browser-local storage and contain the user's unsent repository/task/details; clearing or blocking site storage removes resumability but does not submit the draft.
Idempotency prevents duplicate runs on repeat submission or refresh. Closing a browser does not cancel server work.
Repository discovery reads local metadata and caches only by fingerprint; cache invalidation follows repository fingerprint changes.
No credentials, secrets, fabricated cost/duration, harness identity, competing candidates, or route/cost inputs were added to semantic verification evidence.
Existing run bundles remain readable because the product projection and manifest reference are additive and legacy derivation is conservative.

user_facing_before_after.before: New task exposed the earlier generic run controls and the browser/CLI each interpreted raw controller state. Progress and final output did not have one durable product contract, cancellation/reconnection semantics, or one dominant fail-closed verdict.
user_facing_before_after.after: A configured user selects a repository, writes a multiline task, and presses Run safely. Details holds success criteria, references, attachments, and advanced controls. The same canonical projection drives four truthful stages and one of four verdicts in CLI and web, with unknown accounting preserved and delivery offered only after proved selection.

assumptions: Performance means the strongest currently eligible configured backend before later qualification/routing milestones exist.
No default wall-time means null/absent, not zero or an implicit controller timeout.
High-confidence repository discovery may proceed without confirmation; materially uncertain discovery asks one plain-language question.
Alternative acceptance evidence can support a run when repository validation is unavailable, but absence of sufficient alternative evidence cannot produce Ready to apply.
